output_preview:
Not exactly.

In MCP terms, the server’s capability here is basically “this server exposes tools.” The finer-grained split we were discussing is not a core MCP protocol capability; it’s an application

input_preview: 另外有个旧的 open request 卡： | f6712f02 | [FEATURE] 跨 Session 代码变更记忆同步机制 | ✅ 已落地(GEMINI.md铁律+save_memory+p; 那是在mcp里没有暴露吗？; 是antigravity里用的mcp; Tachi的MCP是不是暴露的tools太多了？; 那你觉得要怎么分一下级别呢？; is this the capability of the mcp?; whats the best use case? or what is the best practice.
output_preview:
Best practice is: expose the smallest tool surface that matches the client’s job.

For an MCP server like Tachi, I would not make every client talk to the same “full power” endpoint. The clean pattern

input_preview: 另外有个旧的 open request 卡： | f6712f02 | [FEATURE] 跨 Session 代码变更记忆同步机制 | ✅ 已落地(GEMINI.md铁律+save_memory+p; 那是在mcp里没有暴露吗？; 是antigravity里用的mcp; Tachi的MCP是不是暴露的tools太多了？; 那你觉得要怎么分一下级别呢？; is this the capability of the mcp?; whats the best use case? or what is the best practice.; and also I have designed a hub for skills and packs.; hmmm think harder
output_preview:
The harder answer is: once you have a Hub, the right split is not “which profile gets which random tools.” It is “which plane is this API in?”

Right now Tachi is mixing at least 4 planes into one MCP
